observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee and adopt the
procedural guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to complete the conduct of
physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) for the One-Time Cleansing of
PPE account balances of Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks every year-end in
time for the closing of the books and submit to the Municipal Accountant;
b. Municipal Accountant to restore to the Cash in Bank and proper liability accounts the
amount corresponding to the unreleased checks at year-end; and
c. Municipal Accountant to adjust all valid reconciling items determined during the
preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements such as stale checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure
that the succeeding receipt of funds related to the disaster risk reduction and
management program shall be recorded in the Trust Liability - DRRM account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make
necessary journal entry to correct the noted misclassification of accounts relative to the
funds received from PCSO and ensure that the succeeding receipt of funds from various
government agencies are recorded in their corresponding account appropriately.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal Development
Council to:
a. assess the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current needs
of the constituents of those unimplemented projects; and
b. ensure optimal and timely utilization of 20 per cent Development Fund in the
succeeding periods by including development projects that are well-planned and
procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Council to include PPAs in the LDRRMFIP that can strengthen the capacity
of the Municipality to build a disaster resilient community and enhance disaster
preparedness and response and accelerate their implementation within the year as
planned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the designated GSO to consolidate all
of the subsequent Requisition and Issue Slip by preparing the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued and then forward the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis for
recording the expense relative to the issued supplies and materials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee to send written invitations to at least three (3) observers
in all stages of the procurement process pursuant to Section 13.1 of 2016 Revised IRR
of RA 9184;
b. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the noted lacking
documentary requirements and the BAC to scrutinize and check the submitted
documents and eligibility requirements of the bidders specifically the noted
deficiencies pursuant to pertinent provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
c. BAC and its Secretariat to submit the copies of perfected Contracts/POs and their
supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution/ issuance thereof
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate
the return of the remaining unutilized/unused fund to the source agency and ensure that
all subsequent unused balance of funds received from any national government agencies
shall be return immediately after the completion of the project pursuant to COA Circular
No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator to submit at the beginning of the year to the Audit
Team the lists of all on-going government projects/ programs/activities (PPA) and those
that are to be implemented during the year in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to restore to the Cash in Bank and proper liability accounts the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks amounting to P9,177,905.78 and credit memo amounting to P236,137.00 in the absence of any document or data on the credit memo in the Trust Fund account.
• Municipal Accountant to adjust all valid reconciling items such as stale checks.
• Municipal Treasurer to prepare the List of Unreleased Checks as of year-end and submit to the Municipal Accountant;
• Municipal Treasurer to prepare a written confirmation/inquiry with the depository bank as to the source and nature of the fund amounting to P5,600.00 in the absence of any document or data on the credit memo in the Trust Fund account.
observations.managementAction: No preparation and non-submission of List of Unreleased Checks as of year-end by the Municipal Treasurer. Reiterated under Observation No. 2, Part II, Page No. 44 of this Report. The fund amounting to P236,137.00 was recorded in the Trust Fund book on February 5, 2021 under JEV No. 30020210201. The Acting Municipal Treasurer has just assumed on his office in September 2021 hence this was not done due to other workload priority. The fund amounting to P5,600.00 was recorded in the book under JEV Nos. 300202104-035 and 300202104-036 dated April 22, 2021 and 300202109-90C dated September 30, 2021.
observations.reason: Non preparation and non-submission of List of Unreleased Checks as of year-end by the Municipal Treasurer. The Acting Municipal Treasurer has just assumed on his office in September 2021 hence this was not done due to other workload priority. No adjustment on stale checks was made as of December 31, 2021. The fund amounting to P236,137.00 was recorded in the Trust Fund book on February 5, 2021 under JEV No. 30020210201. The fund amounting to P5,600.00 was recorded in the book under JEV Nos. 300202104-035 and 300202104-036 dated April 22, 2021 and 300202109-90C dated September 30, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the designated General Services Officer to ensure coordination towards the full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting of all procured supplies and materials and relief goods for distribution and consumption pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has started coursing through the Inventory account the purchases of supplies and materials and relief goods in the latter part of 2021.
observations.reason: Preparation of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) was not accomplished by the designated GSO, hence, full implementation of the perpetual inventory method was not attained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the appropriation of funds in the ensuing year for filling up the position of GSO to address the recurring issues on property management of the Municipality and facilitate the creation of Inventory Committee and that the Inventory Committee and General Services Officer prioritize the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with COA Circular 92-386.
observations.managementAction: A copy of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property was submitted to the Audit team on March 3, 2022. Acting Municipal Treasurer informed that they will comply and that the designated GSO was not able to adopt the guidelines on the one-time cleansing of the PPE account balance due to other workload priority.
observations.reason: The designated GSO was not able to fully implement due to other workload priority as Acting Municipal Treasurer; disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the existing rules and regulations was not given attention.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card for the road and its component namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) road drainage and slope protection structures and d) other components/miscellaneous road structures; and
• disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements.
b. designated General Services Officer to:
• prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network in the prescribed format; and
• maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its component.
c. Municipal Engineer to prepare the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken; and
d. Municipal Accountant and designated GSO to secure from the Municipal Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The personnel in the Accounting Office informed that they have started the preparation of the complete Local Road Network Ledger Card. Awaiting for the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken by the Municipality from the Office of the Municipal Engineer as basis in recording. No report/information was provided by the Office of the Municipal Engineer to the respective Offices.
observations.reason: The necessary complete description and cost segregation of road components was not provided by the Office of the Municipal Engineer; hence, the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Property Card could not be fully prepared and recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the on-going projects under Construction in Progress account based on the Statement of Work Accomplished as of the given period and recognize the corresponding Accounts Payable only upon submission of the Certificate of Completion and Billing Statements, and the Accounts Payable be recorded based on the Certificate of Completion and Billing Statements as basis of recording Accounts Payable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant records the ongoing projects under its Construction in Progress account based on the Statement of Work Accomplished as of the given period and ensures that there is Certificate of Completion and Billing Statements as basis of recording Accounts Payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the unexpended balance of the 30% QRF amounting to P1,881,176.20 and 70% MF-MOOE amounting to P1,670,121.13 in the Trust Fund book on January 5, 2021 under JEV No. 300202101004.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance of the 30% QRF amounting to P1,881,176.20 and 70% MF-MOOE amounting to P1,670,121.13 was recorded in the Trust Fund Book on January 5, 2021 under JEV No. 300202101004.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the transactions and utilization of Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund book in accordance with the provisions of Chapter 4, Volume I of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensures and observes strict compliance with the guidelines in their transactions and utilization of Trust Liability-DRRMF in the Trust Fund book in accordance with Chapter 4, Volume I of the GAM for LGUs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to acknowledge all subsequent cash donations through the issuance of official receipt pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002.
b. Designated Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and designated General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and the related documents, and furnish/provide the Audit Team of the one-time report required to be submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) pursuant to COA Circular 2020-009 and for transparency and accountability over donated cash and in-kind entrusted by the donors to the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer acknowledges the funds received through the issuance of official receipt. The MSWDO submitted the photocopy of ID/certification of the 14 beneficiaries from Barangay Balagbag and provided certification stating that the 25 beneficiaries with inconsistent names appearing in the payroll, SAC form and photos are the same person/individual and assured the audit team that the listed beneficiaries received the cash assistance as these were facilitated by LGUs and representatives from other government agencies such as PNP and DSWD. The MSWDO provided any proof that the 21 beneficiaries are the same person/individual and informed that the names on the one payroll were based on the valid IDs submitted by the beneficiaries. The Municipal Treasurer acknowledged the funds received through the issuance of official receipt.
observations.reason: The one-time report required to be submitted to the NDRRMC, through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) pursuant to COA Circular 2020-009 was not submitted. Some documentary requirements were inconsistent but explanations and supporting documents (IDs, certifications, photos) were provided by MSWDO; hence partial implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant to submit noted lacking documents to the Audit Team and provide explanation/justification on the noted inconsistencies and deficiencies on the supporting documents attached on the Disbursement Vouchers and ensure that the subsequent emergency procurements were in compliance with the documentary requirements required under the existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the noted lacking supporting documents such as ITR and Omnibus Sworn Statement. The Municipal Accountant and the BAC ensured that the subsequent emergency procurements were supported with the required documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to submit the photocopy of ID/certification of the 14 beneficiaries and photos of the 3,915 beneficiaries and ensure the completeness of all the documentary requirements in the subsequent programs similar in nature;
b. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to provide any proof or certification that the 25 beneficiaries with inconsistent names appearing in the payroll, SAC form and photos are the same person/individual and ensure the consistency of records/documents in the subsequent programs similar in nature; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all succeeding receipt of funds be duly acknowledged by issuing Official Receipt pursuant to PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO and Municipal Accountant submitted the photocopy of ID/certification of the 14 beneficiaries from Barangay Balagbag. MSWDO provided certification that the 25 beneficiaries with inconsistent names are the same person and that the listed beneficiaries received the cash assistance as these were facilitated by LGUs and other government agencies. The Municipal Treasurer acknowledged the funds received through issuance of official receipts. The MSWDO informed that some beneficiaries' SAC forms were not updated due to the immediate implementation of the program to meet distribution deadlines and some supporting documents (spouses, household members) explained the discrepancies.
observations.reason: Some SAC forms and records were not fully updated and there were inconsistencies in names due to spouses or household members; immediate implementation to meet distribution deadlines caused some documentation gaps. Municipal Treasurer now issues official receipts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to ensure timely programming of the previous year’s unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be taken into consideration pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include all utilization in the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Council included their CY 2021 available funds in the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund. The unexpended balance of P5,878,786.98 in the Special Trust Fund had P2,242,094.49 included in the LDRRMFIP while P3,636,692.49 was not planned/programmed.
observations.reason: Out of the P5,878,786.98 available funds in the Special Trust Fund in CY 2021, only P2,242,094.49 were programmed/included in the LDRRMFIP; the remaining P3,636,692.49 was not included/planned, hence not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Development Council headed by the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20%DF in the succeeding periods through review, reevaluation and reassessment of its planned PPAs and ensure procurement-and-implementation-ready projects are included.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council undertook review and assessment of the planned PPAs and included procurement-and-implementation-ready projects for subsequent periods. The management informed that 11 out of 33 unimplemented projects were already completed by the Municipality as of May 2022.
observations.reason: Inclusion of procurement-and-implementation-ready projects was not fully achieved in CY 2021 resulting to 33 PPAs not implemented as of December 31, 2021; some projects were completed subsequently (11 out of 33 as of May 2022).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize liability accounts when the goods and services are accepted or supplier/creditor bills are received and to record Construction in Progress based on the Statement of Work Accomplished and close to appropriate asset account as soon as the project is completed pursuant to NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented as full due to timing. The Municipal Accountant recognized ongoing projects as Construction in Progress and recorded the corresponding liability accounts based on contract amount instead of actual accomplishment. Management indicated adjustments should be made when projects are accepted and billing statements are received.
observations.reason: Recording of ongoing projects and liability accounts was based on whole contract cost instead of statement of work accomplished. Contractors' certificates of completion and billing statements were not available; recommendation reiterated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the taxes withheld to be simultaneously set up with the setting up of accounts payable for goods received or services rendered, inspected and accepted, and the corresponding remittances be made in accordance with Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 and BIR Memorandum Circular No. 10-2018.
observations.managementAction: Noted that meeting with local and national government was undertaken first to discuss this matter and decisions/actions will follow. Remittances were processed subsequently.
observations.reason: There was a need to coordinate/meet with local and national government; actions were delayed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer the reconciliation of inter-agency accounts on a quarterly basis or as often as necessary, and prepare a request letter addressed to the Provincial Government requesting for the data/information showing the amount collected in favor of the Municipality to serve as a basis in recording Due from LGUs before closing book of accounts as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer and requested data/information; however, the books of the Accounting Office were already closed when they received the Statement of Account from the Provincial Treasurer regarding share on advance payment of RPT, hence not taken up in the books as of year-end.
observations.reason: Preparation of request letter and reconciliation was not given much attention; books closed before receiving Provincial data.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

accounts the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks amounting to
₱9,177,905.78 in compliance with the GAM for LGUs;

totalling ₱107,806.35 and credit memo amounting to ₱236,137.00 that require
adjustment and correction in the General Ledger that was determined during the

• Municipal Treasurer to prepare the List of Unreleased Checks as of year-end and

• Municipal Treasurer to prepare a written confirmation/inquiry with the
depository bank as to the source and nature of the fund amounting to ₱5,600.00 in
the absence of any document or data on the credit memo in the Trust Fund account.

account balances of the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee and

its component namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope

Statement of Work Accomplished as of the given period and recognize the
corresponding Accounts Payable only upon submission of the Certificate of
Completion and Billing Statements pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for

record the unexpended balance of the 30% QRF amounting to ₱1,881,176.20 and 70%
MF-MOOE amounting to ₱1,670,121.13 of the current year LDRRMF in the Trust Fund
books under the Trust Liability-DRRM account at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
record the transactions and utilization of Trust Liability- DRRM in the Trust Fund book
in accordance with the provisions of Chapter 4, Volume I of the Government
Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to acknowledge all subsequent cash donations through
the issuance of official receipt pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002;
b. Designated Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and
designated General Services Officer to prepare and submit the Summary/List
of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and the related documents,
and furnish/provide the Audit Team of the one-time report required to be
submitted to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council
(NDRRMC), through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) pursuant to COA
Circular 2020-009 and for transparency and accountability over donated cash
and in kind entrusted by the donors to the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee
and Municipal Accountant to submit noted lacking documents to the Audit Team and
provide explanation/justification on the noted inconsistencies and deficiencies on the
supporting documents attached on the Disbursement Vouchers and ensure that the
subsequent emergency procurements were in compliance with the documentary
requirements required under the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer
to submit the noted lacking documents such as the photocopy of
ID/certification of the 14 beneficiaries from Barangay Balagbag and photos of
the actual receipt of the assistance of the 429 beneficiaries and ensure the
completeness of all the documentary requirements in the subsequent
programs similar in nature;
b. Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to provide any proof or
certification that the 25 beneficiaries with inconsistent names appearing in the
payroll, SAC form and photos are one and the same person or members of one
family and ensure consistency of records/documents in the subsequent
programs similar in nature; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all succeeding receipt of funds be duly
acknowledged by issuing Official Receipt pursuant to the PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to ensure timely
programming of the previous year’s unexpended balance of the LDRRMF be taken
into consideration pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the MDRRMO, to include all of the utilization in the Report on the
Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Municipal
Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund in the
succeeding periods through review, reevaluation and reassessment of its planned
PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to present the Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Investments account with maturity date of less than three months or 90 days under Cash and Cash Equivalents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant presented and classified its high yield savings account with maturity date of less than three months or 90 days as Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Time Deposits totaling ₱7,181,302.23 as of December 31, 2020 under Cash and Cash Equivalents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the list of actual Delinquent Real Property Tax per taxpayers and the corresponding amount due and collectible for the Delinquent Real Property Tax from CYs 1986 to 2008; b. Municipal Accountant to book up the unrecorded RPT/SET Receivables in the Municipality’s books of accounts and refrain from making direct adjustments on the said receivable account without proper documentation as to name of taxpayers and the corresponding amount subject for adjustment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer provided the Municipal Accountant the list of Delinquent Real Property Tax per taxpayers of year 1986 to 2008. The list of Delinquent Real Property Tax per taxpayers totaling ₱298,934.47 was maintained by the Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Accountant determined that the direct adjustment on the receivable account was not due to lack of supporting documents.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the RPT Delinquent per Municipal records was not undertaken

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Assessor, designated General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to determine the nature of acquisition of unrecorded lands, conduct site inspection and prepare reports on the actual usage of each parcels of land and existence of unlawful occupants, if any; b. After determining the nature of acquisition, recognize in the Municipality’s books the twenty-two (22) parcels of land using the market value indicated in the earliest applicable Tax Declarations under the name of the Municipality, and make the proper disclosure on the Notes to the Financial Statements on the actual usage of each parcels of land; c. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to process the necessary documents and register the land owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System; and d. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to determine the intention on the Municipality’s counterpart on the acquired lot by the Barangay Government of Poblacion VI, whether it was donated to the Barangay or had an authority to use of the said lot only, and make proper adjustment or disclosure in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor, designated General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant determined the nature of acquisition of the acquired land and identified the counterpart of the Municipality on the lot in the Barangay Poblacion VI amounting to ₱50,000.00 be classified as donation to the Barangay Poblacion VI. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to process the necessary documents for registration of the land were not yet acted upon due to lack of time because of the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Identification of nature of acquisition of the land is yet to be determined while the report on the actual usage of each parcels of land was not yet acted upon. Processing of documents and registration not focused due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated General Services Officer to: a. maintain Property Cards for each category of PPE using the format prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs and assign property number for each property; b. locate and secure PPE totaling the missing properties and if whereabouts cannot be determined after exerting all efforts, require the persons accountable to prepare the Incident report indicating the reasons of missing properties and if found negligent they shall pay the missing properties at cost; and c. update the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the existing rules and regulations. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount of property, plant and equipment retired from active use and held for disposal, and those properties of which existence was not established as of year-end. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the present organizational structure of the Municipality and require the Municipal Budget Officer to analyze and consider the appropriation of funds in the ensuing year for the filling up of the General Service Officer.
observations.managementAction: The designated GSO informed that they have started the updating of the Property Cards of the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer acknowledged that the GSO could not focus on the updating of Property Cards due to other workload in the Office of the Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Budget Officer informed that the recommendation will be implemented and included in their priorities once the situations normalize because they are currently focusing on the combat against COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: There was lack of time due to Taal Eruption and COVID-19 pandemic. The designated GSO and Municipal Treasurer could not focus on the updating of Property Cards and disposal due to other workload priorities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make proper adjustments in the books and ensure that the subsequent shares from the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) - Lotto Draws and Small Town Lottery be appropriately accounted as Share from PCSO account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the proper adjustments in the Trust Fund books and recognized the amounts received in CY 2020 under the Share from PCSO account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor devise the guidelines for the implementation of cash assistance to indigents and endorse to the Sangguniang Bayan so that the latter can pass a resolution adopting said guidelines to improve efficiency and effectiveness of the assistance implementation.
observations.managementAction: The guidelines for cash assistance such as medical assistance, burial assistance and educational assistance were endorsed to the Sangguniang Bayan and a resolution adopting the guidelines was passed on March 9, 2021 (SB Resolution No. 2021-82 dated March 16, 2021).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct pre-bid conference for projects with ABC above ₱1,000,000.00 and scrutinize the submitted documents of the prospective bidders and ensure compliance with the eligibility requirements. Also require BAC to submit justification why contracts are awarded to bidders in spite of non-conformance with eligibility requirements.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted corrected documents, conducted scrutiny and submitted justification for certain awards. However, some clarification and compliance remain outstanding.
observations.reason: Submission of supporting documents and full justification on non-conformance was not given priority and some items were not fully scrutinized before award.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Municipal Mayor: a. revisit, re-evaluate and reassess the projects in the Development Fund and those projects not allowed to be charged against the fund; and b. ensure that all programs, projects and activities funded out of 20% Development Fund are in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 and are optimally utilized.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council evaluated the projects and the total amount of ₱8,518,739.27 worth of projects were evaluated; projects from Continuing Appropriations amounted to ₱5,518,739.27 were from the Continuing Appropriations and some projects were revised and reverted; optimal utilization was not attained due to COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Optimal utilization of the fund was not attained due to the COVID-19 pandemic and constraints in implementation of infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP for the unexpended LDRRMF in the Trust Fund book and ensure optimal utilization and implementation of programs and projects funded out of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO prepared and submitted the LDRRMFIP and the Supplemental LDRRMFIP was approved (SB Resolution No. 2020-85 dated April 7, 2020 and SB Resolution No. 2020-244 dated October 13, 2020). Out of the available fund, ₱7,186,191.16 was included and ₱7,100,796.00 was utilized. Some Continuing Appropriation funds were utilized proportionally.
observations.reason: Out of the available fund in the Continuing Appropriation of ₱4,389,411.13 only 33.97% or ₱1,490,954.50 were utilized; some projects subject to implementation timelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strengthen the Municipality’s GFPS by completing the composition of the GFPS Executive Committee and Technical Working Group as required by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS commenced on the composition and the Technical Working Group implementation was not fully undertaken in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Commencement of GFPS and composition work started; full implementation requires more time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the GAD Focal Point System and its Technical Working Group to establish and maintain their GAD Database pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-1.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point Person informed that they are doing their best to maintain the GAD database and that some data collection was undertaken from different offices but full maintenance not yet achieved.
observations.reason: Implementation ongoing and requires more time before database fully established and operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to: a. conduct the gender analysis of programs and projects of the Municipality using the HGDG Tool; and b. ensure that at least five (5) percent of the total budget is gender-responsive pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-1 and Section 43 of RA No. 9710.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point Person informed that they are exerting efforts to conduct gender analysis based on available data and that supplemental budget considerations were being made; however, full integration and 5% allocation not yet achieved.
observations.reason: GAD analysis being done with best effort; data limitations and immediate priorities affected attainment of the 5% allocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to always secure the Certificate of Endorsement of the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget from the GPB of the Department of Interior and Local Government as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Officials acknowledged the recommendation and noted that the Certificate of Endorsement from DILG for the CY 2020 GAD Plan and Budget was not obtained; the process will be pursued.
observations.reason: Certificate of Endorsement was not obtained; issuance and follow-up pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
present the high yield savings account with maturity date of less than three months
or 90 days under Cash and Cash Equivalents instead of Investment pursuant to
PPSAS 2.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
provide the Municipal Accountant the list of actual Delinquent Real Property Tax per
taxpayers and the corresponding amount due and collectible for CYs 1986 to 2008
and the latter to book up the unrecorded RPT/SET Receivables in the Municipality’s
books of accounts and refrain from making direct adjustments on the said receivable
account without proper documentation as to name of taxpayers and the
corresponding amount subject for adjustment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Assessor, designated General Services Officer and Municipal
Accountant to determine the nature of acquisition of unrecorded lands, either
through sale or donations, to establish the basis on the valuation;
b. Designated General Services Officer and Municipal Assessor to conduct site
inspection and prepare the reports on the actual usage of each parcels of land
and existence of unlawful occupants, if any, and provide the said report to the
Municipal Accountant;
c. After determining the nature of acquisition, require the Municipal Accountant to
recognize in the Municipality’s books of accounts the twenty-two (22) parcels of
land using the market value indicated in the earliest applicable Tax Declarations
under the name of the Municipality, and make the proper disclosure on the Notes
to the Financial Statements on the actual usage of each parcels of land based on
the report to be prepared by the Municipal Assessor and designated General
Services Officer;
d. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to process the necessary documents
and register the land owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System; and
e. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to determine the intention on the
Municipality’s counterpart on the acquired lot by the Barangay Government of
Poblacion VI, whether it was donated to the Barangay or had an authority to use of
the said lot only, and make proper adjustment or disclosure in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated General Services
Officer to:
a. maintain Property Cards for each category of PPE using the format prescribed
in the NGAS Manual for LGUs and assign property number for each property;
b. locate and secure PPE totaling ₱3,423,240.24, and if whereabouts cannot be
determined after exerting all efforts, require the persons accountable to
prepare the incident report indicating the reasons of missing properties and if
found negligent they shall pay the missing properties at cost; and
c. update the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties
(IIRUP) and facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties in
accordance with the existing rules and regulations.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount of property, plant and
equipment retired from active use and held for disposal, and those properties of
which existence was not established as of year-end.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor evaluate the present
organizational structure of the Municipality and require the Municipal Budget Officer
to analyze and consider the appropriation of funds in the ensuing year for the filling up of
the General Service Officer to have a permanent official who will assume the duties and
responsibilities in the office considering the complexities and perennial issues that needs
to be addressed on the supply and property management of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
recognize liability when the goods and services are accepted or rendered and
supplier/creditor bills are received and take up in the books the agency assets and
public infrastructures as "Construction in Progress” with the appropriate asset
classification during the construction period based on Statement of Work
Accomplished, and closed to appropriate asset account as soon as the project is
completed pursuant to NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
recognize in the books the taxes withheld simultaneously with the setting up of
accounts payable (AP) for goods received or services rendered, inspected and
accepted, and for construction/infrastructure projects partially or fully completed and
inspected pursuant to Revenue Regulation No. 2-98, as amended and clarified under
BIR Memorandum Circular No. 10-2018 dated January 31, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and
Provincial Treasurer the reconciliation of inter-agency accounts on a quarterly basis
or as often as necessary, and prepare a request letter address to the Provincial
Government requesting for the data/information showing the amount collected in
favor of the Municipality to serve as a basis in recording Due from LGUs before
closing book of accounts as of year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to make
proper adjustment in the books and ensure that the subsequent shares from the
Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) - Lotto Draws and Small Town Lottery
(STL) of the Municipality is appropriately accounted as income and recorded using
“Share from PCSO (4-04-01-020)” account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the management devise the implementing guidelines for cash
assistance to indigents and endorse to the Sangguniang Bayan so that the latter can
pass a resolution adopting in full or with modification the said guidelines to improve
efficiency and effectiveness of the project implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee
to conduct pre-bid conference for projects with an ABC above ₱1,000,000.00 and
scrutinize the submitted documents of the prospective bidders and ensure that the
recommended contractors for awarding of contracts are eligible and compliant with the
eligibility requirements pursuant to 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the Bids
and Awards Committee to submit justification why the contracts are awarded to the
bidders in spite non-conformance with the eligibility requirements as mandated by
law.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Municipal
Mayor revisit, re-evaluate and reassess the projects in the Continuing Appropriations
and those projects not allowed to be charged against the fund which are remained
unimplemented as of year-end so as to align to the current needs of the constituents
and ensure that all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) funded out of 20 percent
Development Fund was in accordance with the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 and
optimally utilize for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the designated Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer to prepare and submit the required
LDRRMFIP specifying in details the programs, projects and activities funded out of
unexpended LDRRMF in the Trust Fund book pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Likewise, ensure the optimal utilization and implementation of the programs and
projects funded out of Disaster Mitigation Fund and Continuing Appropriation within
the planned timeline.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strengthen the Municipality’s GFPS by
completing the composition of the GFPS Executive Committee and Technical Working
Group as required by the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and the GAD Focal Point System and its Technical Working
Group to establish and maintain GAD Database pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System
Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to conduct the gender analysis
on the programs and projects of the Municipality using the Harmonized Gender and
Development Guidelines (HGDG) Tool and ensure that at least five (5) percent of the
programs, projects and activities of the Municipality’s total budget is gender-
responsive pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular 2016-1
and Section 43 of Republic Act No. 9710.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GFPS Executive Committee
and Technical Working Group to always ensure that the Certificate of Endorsement of
the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget from the Department of Interior and Local
Government be secured pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01 as amended
by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to verify the existing
discrepancies on the recorded real properties of taxpayers assessed and due
for assessment, if any;
b. Municipal Treasurer to constantly provide the Municipal Accountant the
certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and collectible
for the year which shall become the latter’s basis for setting up tax
receivables; and
c. Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Administrator and Municipal Assessor to
continuously adopt strategies/measures to collect delinquent taxes.

Management’s Comments:
The Municipal Treasurer replied that they are continuously reconciling and exerting extra
efforts to find the existing discrepancies on the recorded real properties of taxpayers
assessed and due for assessment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Designated LDRRM Officer to comply with Section 5.1.2 and Section 5.1.15 of
COA Circular No. 2012-002 in preparing and submission of LDRRMFIP for
unexpended QRF and MOOE of previous years.
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and effect adjusting entries for the transfer
of CY 2012 unexpended QRF from Trust Fund to the unappropriated surplus
of the municipality.
c. LDRRM Council to adopt adequate planning to identify timely, urgent, feasible
and attainable projects and activities to achieve the objectives of the DRRM
Fund allocation. Consider in planning all pertinent factors which could
probably affect the timeliness of delivery and full accomplishment of the
identified projects and activities.
d. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Office of the DRRMO for the
recording of the received supplies and equipment from USAID/World Food
Programme in the books of the Municipality.

Management’s Comments:
As of this writing, the audit team did not receive any written reply from the management.
Although, during the exit conference, the MDRRMO Designate stated that the audit
team’s recommendations were well taken. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant stated
that they will record in the books the received supplies and equipment from
USAID/World Food Programme upon availability of the cost of those items and that they
will transfer to the unappropriated surplus the CY 2012 unexpended QRF from Trust
Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to
adopt strategies to closely monitor collections, consistently update records/ledger
of stall occupants and verify accuracy of such, execute a contract of lease
embodied in a public instrument for every public market stall occupant and lastly,
to strictly enforce its Market Code, especially in collecting delinquent rentals and
enforcement of surcharges, in order to generate additional income that will be
used for the implementation of other projects/programs of the municipality.

Management’s Comments:
During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer forwarded an updated list of
stallholders and corresponding collectibles from them. This has to be undertaken by the
audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Engineering and MPDC Office to ensure the effective and efficient
implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion
period to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment
thereof by the intended beneficiaries. Likewise, ensure strict observance of
the guidelines in the planning, identification and utilization of the fund in
accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.
b. Ensure that a MOA between Municipality and barangays or any other agency
is executed prior any fund transfer implementation.

Management’s Comment:
The audit team has not yet received any written reply from the management pertaining
to this audit observation.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board review and
update its Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the current and succeeding
years in compliance with Section 16 of RA 9003.
a. Include an action plan on the rehabilitation of the old sanitary landfill and
Ecological Resource management Center.
b. Review the initial plan of establishing a new sanitary landfill considering the
budgetary requirements and other factors which may affect its feasibility and
necessity, otherwise, if proven not feasible and/or adequate relative to the
existing circumstances, the municipality may consider other solid waste
management programs provided that it was convened and approved through
a Resolution.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Government ensure that all
barangays have established its own operational MRF, commence with the
operation of using the vermi-compost facility constructed in Brgy. Bungahan and
immediately act on the bidding for hauling of the municipality’s accumulated
wastes.

Management’s Comment:
As of this writing, the management have not yet responded on the audit observation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the BAC to require the
contractors to post sufficient warranty security of 30% of total contract price in
case of issuing surety bonds callable upon demand. Likewise, we recommend that
the Municipal Government through the Municipal Engineer ensure that all relevant
information on signages and public notice of PPAs be provided as required by
COA Circular 2013-004.

Management’s Comments:
No written reply was forwarded by the management to the audit team with regards to this
audit observation. Although during exit conference, the BAC Chairman expressed
affirmation on the recommendation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal GAD Focal
Point System to convene on the setting up and maintaining a GAD database to
comply with the provision of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2013-01. Likewise, ensure that the program/projects/activities be supported
with gender analysis and identified gender issues and concerns. Furthermore, in
preparation of GAD Plan and Budget, indicate the specific and quantified
performance target of GAD Activity in order to evaluate the effectiveness of the
municipality’s accomplishments.

Management’s Comments:
The MSWDO commented that they will request for the setting up of the GAD database
to comply with the provision of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01 since the municipality is only using the sex-disaggregated data of every
department as basis in the planning and formulation of GAD Plan and Budget.
On the issue of granting financial assistance not considered to be gender related, she
detailed that 758 of the 1,227 beneficiaries were women who are considered more
affected in the financial limitation of their family since they are the one who manages the
income even if they are also working.
She also added that they will input the specific and quantified target data of our
beneficiaries in the next formulation of GAD Plan and Budget. Though, she mentioned
that they believed that the GAD Plan and Budget was reviewed and approved by the
DILG and Provincial Planning and Development Office but does not have comments and
recommendation for that topic.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Audit Team did not discount the review made by PPDO and DILG.
Recommendations were based on the mentioned criteria from the Joint Memorandum
Circular which will aid the Municipality in evaluating its GAD accomplishments. Likewise,
the identification of GAD Projects/Programs and Activities should always be based on
gender mainstreaming and analysis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
exert further efforts to communicate with GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PHILHEALTH to
confirm the balances and/or to check with the records whether all premiums/loan
repayments/contribution deducted from the payroll of employees and Government
Share (GS) for all employees were remitted in order to identify the affected
employees on the unremitted balance, if any. Otherwise, if the mentioned agencies
were able to confirm that they have no outstanding claims from the Municipality,
request for the reversion of such payables following the applicable guidelines.

Management Comments:
The Municipal Accountant stated during the exit conference that the said observation
had been a recurring audit finding. He also stated that he had already talked with the
former Supervising Auditor about this concern several years ago. He stated that those
recorded payables were already verified with the respective agencies and have
appeared that those have been already paid by the Municipality. He mentioned that he
will take into consideration the recommendation given by the audit team as to the
reversion of such payables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to conduct physical inventory regularly and ensure that all acquired in recent years be properly monitored. We recommended the Municipal Treasurer that the Accounting used the net book value for unserviceable assets while the inventory was based on historical cost.
b) Municipal Accountant to document every process made in the identification of all properties recorded in their books of accounts, and if unaccounted or unidentified items still persists, then seek the assistance of proper authority to drop from the books of accounts the items that cannot be identified.
To ensure the accuracy, reliability and fair presentation, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule consistent with the balances per financial statements and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses for the assets reported.
We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a) the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in updating the inventory of unserviceable properties and exclude them from the report of serviceable PPE and prioritize the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the afore cited rules and regulations so that the balances of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts presented in the financial statements would only represent those assets that are in existence and servicing the needs of the Municipality.
b) Accountable personnel to refund the market value of those unserviceable properties that are lost/missing.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer had conducted physical inventory of all properties. The Municipal Accountant was reconciled with that of the Municipal Treasurer’s inventory. A journal entry was made in May 23, 2018 recording adjustment on the depreciation of 2017. The Municipal Accountant prepared an inventory list of the disposal of the municipalities recorded the unserviceable assets. The Municipal Accountant records of the identification was reconciled with that of the Municipal Treasurer’s inventory.
observations.reason: There was no response yet on the Municipality’s recorded unserviceable properties. The Municipal Treasurer had prepared an inventory list of the disposal of the municipalities unserviceable assets. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page No.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the certified list of taxpayers with the corresponding amount due and collectible for the CY 2018 and years thereafter pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units, Volume 1; and
b. direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Administrator and Municipal Assessor to jointly exert extra efforts in the collection of delinquent accounts and enable the maximun of collection of unpaid taxes; and
c. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer did not furnish the Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with Cy 2018. Comparison of the balances of delinquent current and prior year collections of delinquent accounts disclosed that there is a decrease of 425,641.48 which is a sign of improvement of collection efficiency. The two Municipal Offices have started reconciling the accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to provide certified list of tax receivables to Accounting at the beginning of the year 2018. The Municipal Assessor’s list was used thinking that it is also the same with that of the Municipal Treasurer’s list.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) and in the required pro-forma Notes to Financial Statements prescribed in Section No. 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The management averred that justified that any misclassification of activities in cash flows is due to the initial disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements. Those details of items not disclosed in the Notes to FS were presented in separate supporting schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the balances of the Due to and Due from LGUs (Province), Due from/Due to Other Funds and Due to/Due from accounts by identifying the causes of discrepancies and displaying net discrepancies for adjustment, if warranted, particularly at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the corresponding accounts. There is a decrease in discrepancy. On the other hand for the balances of Due to and Due from LGU (Province) no discrepancy.
observations.reason: The rest is still reconciliation. Previous years’ balances remained unremitted due to lacking supporting documents. Reconciliation is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record inventory for medical supplies, drugs and medicines purchased for operations as inventory accounts and credit the same in the appropriate accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009. For those funded from the PhilHealth’s PFPR, draw a corollary entry on the General Fund upon purchase and issuances. Likewise, reconcile balances with the physical count of stocks.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant commented that the recording inventory for purchases for operations as inventory account will result to possible overstatement of inventories and on expenses dependent upon the accounts to be used. The Municipal Accountant reconciled the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant there had been reconciled as year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the record/stock cards and inventory records of the Office Supplies Inventory and record/stock card balance every quarter.
observations.managementAction: Office Supplies Inventory per record/stock cards had been reconciled as year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the financial statements in the compliance with the requirements of the PPSASs including the required Performance reconciliation to be disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts is reconciled with the figures presented in the Statement of Financial Performance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal Development Council, require that the Council include only projects that are feasible and ready for implementation in its Annual Investment Plan and only those ongoing projects for every quarter and report/list of projects for each year and report ongoing projects for every quarter in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: At the beginning of 2019, the audit team did not receive the report/list of projects for particular year. Although the Municipal Engineer submits every quarter, a report on status of implementation of the municipality’s projects.
observations.reason: There was no justification for non-submission at the beginning of the year the report on implementation. The management did not provide justification for non-submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the finishing of the storage building for use by the Accounting Office, and other offices and the audit team in order to accommodate the increasing volume of documents that needs to be secured on the preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.
observations.managementAction: The building has been completed and may now be use as a storage room. The building (Multi Purpose Building) Check No. 1588330 dtd 09/10/18.

observations.recommendation:
Considering the repetitive findings on LDRRM, we recommended that the LDRRM Council headed by the Municipal Mayor:
• require the LDRRM Office of the Municipality and barangays to determine/assess and document the identified disaster risks in the Municipality and barangays to be able to plan disaster-responsive activities and documenting the actual need for the purchase of disaster equipment and supplies; and
• resolve in the upcoming council meeting any necessary amendments/updates to the LDRRM Investment Plan including only programs and activities that the Council intends to implement based on disaster risk assessment and verified information from the LDRRMP on pursuing the purchase activities existing in the plan. Consider providing trainings given the available LDRRMF funds that could better inform and improve disaster resiliency of barangay constituents through the help of barangay officials.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the planned activities and projects of 2018 were diligently planned. The LDRRMO was still to work out the implementation/reprogramming of those previously appropriated in the Continuing Appropriation.
observations.reason: The LDRRMO is still to work out on the update of LDRRMIP. The municipality was not able to optimally utilize its Development Fund due to several reasons including need for special expertise and some projects not feasible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Engineering and MPDC Office to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries. Likewise, ensure strict observance of the guidelines in the planning, identification and utilization of the fund in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1. Also, instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement process considering that Procurement Law allows alternative methods in case of failure of bidding.
observations.managementAction: Out of 33 projects in CY 2018, 20 projects were implemented. The management’s evaluation includes that of contractor’s manpower and equipment utilization. The Municipal is to expedite the procurement process.
observations.reason: Despite prior year observation, the municipality was not able to optimally utilize the 20% Development Fund due to planning and implementation issues and some projects were not allowed to be charged against the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor to refund the amount equivalent to the construction works not performed and finish the project in accordance to the specifications and plan. Otherwise, require the contractor to refund the project amount.
observations.managementAction: In 2018, the improvement of the MRF was done. The hand railing and the three-step concreting of the staircase wall mentioned was not implemented since it is not included in the construction works plan. Same with the painting wherein exterior and interior walls were not painted.
observations.reason: The hand railing and staircase wall concreting was not included in the construction works plan. The painting works were not performed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
a. adhere with the provisions of RA 9184 in posting the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Contract Agreement;
b. validate the contractor’s list of ongoing projects/awarded contractor’s list to check contractor’s eligibility and coordinate/confirm with other agencies/entities; and
c. scrutinize the contractor’s manpower and equipment on all projects to be awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same set of equipment and manpower.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of the Municipal is incorporated in the Annual Procurement Plan. Validation is being done by the BAC to check contractor’s eligibility. The management’s evaluation includes that of contractor’s manpower and equipment utilization.
observations.reason: There was slow internet connection in the area and delays in validation. Some outstanding works per NFCC do not tie up with the actual ongoing projects. There was no noted major unjustified delays, though among projects there were delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Office-Designate to meticulously and judiciously plan procurement for each quarter as consolidated and incorporate them in the Annual Procurement Plan in order to adopt competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance that the best quality at the lowest price is availed.
observations.managementAction: Procurement is incorporated in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of MAO, to present the Breeding Stocks at PPSAS-compliant financial statements and revalue the Holstein cows, gilts and other breeds at fair value less cost to sell at year-end and recognize any gain or loss arising from change in fair value.
observations.managementAction: Implemented for the Holstein cows except for the gilts. Other breeds of cows and gilts were recorded at original acquisition cost.
observations.reason: Only the Holstein is revalued at fair value less cost to sell at year-end. Other breeds were not revalued and still recorded at acquisition cost.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer return to the Accountant the original disbursement vouchers and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the reversing of the stale checks and to ensure that reconciling items are timely adjusted in the Bank Reconciliation Statement to effect accurate and reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustment to reverse the stale checks for years 2014-2016 totaling ₱221,207.00 was recorded in the books. A journal entry was made on February 27, 2018. Another entry was made on February 7, 2019 to correct the account used in reversing the stale checks. The necessary adjusting journal entries were made.
observations.reason: There are remaining stale checks with total amount of ₱58,388.03 used in the books. Another entry was made on February 7, 2019 to correct the account used in reversing the stale checks. There are still unreconciled items in the bank reconciliation statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the formulation and passage of the GAD Code to serve as the framework of the gender-responsive governance and programming of GAD related activities.
observations.managementAction: GAD Code was formulated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to conduct physical inventory regularly and ensure
that all properties counted particularly those acquired in recent years be
properly monitored. We recommended that the difference of ₱551,299.21
be reconciled with the accounting records and be disclosed in the Notes to
Financial Statements
b) Municipal Accountant to document every process made in the
identification of all properties recorded in their books of accounts, and if
unaccounted or unidentified items still persists, then seek the assistance of
proper authority to drop from the books of accounts the items that cannot
be identified.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the lapsing schedule consistent with the balances presented in the financial statements and
provide the corresponding depreciation expenses for the assets reported.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a) the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort in updating the inventory of
unserviceable properties the basis of disposal and exclude them from the
report of serviceable PPE and prioritize the disposal of unserviceable
properties in accordance with the afore cited rules and regulations so that
the balances of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts presented in the
financial statements would only represent those assets that are in
existence and servicing the needs of the Municipality.
b) Accountable personnel to refund the market value of those unserviceable
properties that are lost/missing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant the
certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding amount due and
collectible for the CY 2018 and years thereafter pursuant to Section 20 of
NGAS Manual for Local Government Units, Volume 1;
b. direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Administrator and Municipal
Assessor to coordinate and jointly exert extra efforts in the collection of
delinquent accounts and adopt necessary measures to enable the maximum
collection of unpaid taxes; and
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile
the list of delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded
RPT and SET Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the
financial statements in accordance with the PPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA
Circular No. 2016-004 to assist users in understanding the financial statements and
to provide them with information relevant to decision-making.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile
the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs by identifying the
causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for adjustment, if
warranted, particularly at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to record medical supplies, drugs and medicines purchased for
operations as inventory accounts and credit the same for issuance to end-users,
transfers or other disposal using the appropriate accounts provided in COA
Circular No. 2015-009. For those funded from the Philhealth’s PFPR, draw a
corollary entry on the General Fund upon purchase and issuances. Likewise,
reconcile inventory account balances with the physical count of stocks.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
to reconcile record/stock cards balances every quarter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the financial statements in compliance with the requirements
of PPSAS including the required reconciliation to be disclosed in the Notes to
Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer
and other concerned officials to provide the timing of implementation of projects for
each year and report ongoing projects for each every quarter until they are
completed in compliance with the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the finishing of the
building for use by the Municipal Accounting Office, other offices and the audit
team in order to accommodate the increasing volume of documents that needs to be
secured with consideration on the preservation period of ten (10) years as mandated
by law.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council headed by the Municipal Mayor to:
 require the LDRRMO of the diligent performance of his duties and
responsibilities by determining/assessing and documenting the identified
disaster risks in the Municipality and its barangays to be able to plan
disaster-responsive activities and maximize the annual LDRRMF Plan and
verify the actual need for the purchase of disaster equipment and supplies
allotted with current and continuing appropriations; and
 resolve in the upcoming council meeting any necessary amendments/updates
to the LDRRM Investment Plan including only programs and activities that
the Council intends to implement based on disaster risk assessment and
verified information from the LDRRMP on pursuing the purchase activities
existing in the plan. Consider providing trainings given the available
LDRRMF funds that could better inform and improve disaster resiliency of
barangay constituents through the help of barangay officials;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal
Development Council, require that the Council include only projects that are
feasible and ready for implementation with properly planned timing of
implementation in its Annual Investment Plan and only those among the social,
economic and environmental projects enlisted in DILG-DBM JMC 2017-01. We
also recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement
process considering that Procurement Law allows alternative methods in case of
failure of bidding.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately require the
completion of the project in accordance to the specifications and plan. Otherwise,
require the contractor to refund the amount of construction works not performed
equivalent to the visible structural deficiencies.

In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the
Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to:
a. adhere with the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 in posting the Notice of
Award, Notice to Proceed and Contract Agreement;
b. validate the contractor’s list of on-going projects/awarded not yet started;
coordinate/confirm with other agencies/entities and ensure that all possible
on-going projects/awarded not yet started are included therein. Check,
verify and validate all the eligibility documents submitted by contractors
before awarding projects and document them in a report of post-
qualification; and
c. scrutinize the contractor’s manpower and equipment on all projects to be
awarded and not on an individual project only in cases where the latter is the
lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or overlapping
implementation period. This is in order to require contractor to augment its
manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the project awarded to it or to
disqualify it from award of several contracts beyond its capacity in
consonance with the provisions of Section 23.7.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services
Officer-Designate to meticulously and judiciously plan procurement for each
quarter as consolidated and incorporate them in the Annual Procurement Plan
(APP) in order to adopt competitive public bidding to provide reasonable assurance
that the best quality at the lowest price is availed. Ensure that only those that are
emergency in nature and those ordinary or regular supplies and materials not
available in the Procurement Services (PS) and within the threshold are purchased
through alternative modes of procurement.

Furnish the COA Auditor copy of the approved reports; and  direct the Acting GSO to maintain PPE property cards, and periodic reconciliation of these records with subsidiary ledgers of the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and, as the case may be, the head of the department/office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to record medical supplies, drugs and medicines purchased for operations as inventory accounts and credit the same for issuance to end-users, transfers or other disposal using the appropriate accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009. For those funded from the Philhealth’s PFPR, draw a corollary entry on the General Fund upon purchase and issuances. Likewise, reconcile inventory account balances with the physical count of stocks.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal Accountant commented that recognizing inventory for purchases under Trust Fund will result to possible overstatement in inventories and on expenses dependent upon the accounts to be used. He also reasoned out that the purchases coming from the General Fund is correct and can be accounted using inventory and/or expense account, however, such purchases are directly issued to all barangays and good only for three months.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Assets are defined in PPSAS as resources presently controlled by the entity as a result of past event. The service potential or ability to generate economic benefits can arise directly from the resource itself or from the rights to use the source. PPSAS 12 on Inventories clearly provides that inventories are recognized as assets upon purchase and expense when sold, exchanged and distributed. Regardless of the funding source, all inventory of medicines, supplies and other sorts acquired by the Municipality and which brings economic benefit are considered assets and but proper to be recognized as Inventory in the books. Moreover, recognizing inventory for purchases will not result to overstatement of Inventory account balance in the books as long as utilization is properly monitored and reported to the Accounting Office for the appropriate recognition of expense.

We recommended thatin order for the Management tofully attain the purpose and intent in establishing the 20% Development Fund at the soonest possible time, a) resort to alternative modes of procurement as provided in RA 9184 after having two failed biddings for projects of high priority, b) coordinate with the National Agencies, Barangay or Province which may have projects for implementation that might coincide with the planned project of the Municipality, c) carefully plot the timing of implementation with the availability of IRA.

We recommended that the Municipality not only encourage all the Barangays to promote and support the SWM programs of the Municipality but also monitor their compliance and enforce the appropriate penalties on violators.  Instruct all barangay captains to inculcate in the minds of all their constituents the value of proper solid waste management, enact barangay ordinances relative thereto and strictly implement the provisions thereon.
